arabic_text:
لَا الشَّمْسُ يَنبَغِي لَهَا أَن تُدْرِكَ الْقَمَرَ وَلَا اللَّيْلُ سَابِقُ النَّهَارِ ۚ وَكُلٌّ فِي فَلَكٍ يَسْبَحُونَ
ک ل ف ی ف ل ک — یسبحون
english_text: It is not permissible for the sun to reach the moon, nor does the night overtake the day, but each, in an orbit, is rotating.
alt_text: Quran 36:40 (Sura Ya-Sin) with English translation. Below: a decomposition of the phrase 'كُلٌّ فِي فَلَكٍ يَسْبَحُونَ' showing the letters ک ل ف ی ف ل ک as a palindromic pattern around the letter ی, labelled 'ROTATION' to highlight the linguistic mimesis of the verse's meaning.
notes: Quran 36:40 — celebrated example of Quranic linguistic mimesis: the phrase 'each in an orbit' (kullun fi falakin) is itself a palindrome reading the same forward and backward.
